hero.title: AI-IDEI · Knowledge Operating System
hero.description: Complete architecture of a Knowledge Operating System built on atomic, programmable Neurons. Designed for 100K+ neurons with full-text search, Git-like versioning, dynamic block types, cloning/templates, deterministic AI services, credit economy, real-time notification system, feedback & testimonials, and advanced administration.

s1.title: 1. Concept & Mission
s1.callout: Transforming raw knowledge (transcriptions, notes, ideas) into programmable, capitalizable, and tradeable intellectual assets.
s1.intro: AI-IDEI is a Knowledge Operating System (KOS) — not a simple note editor or CMS. The system operates on the principle that every knowledge unit (Neuron) is a programmable object that can be:
bullets: Structured — into typed blocks with semantic metadata
Executed — through deterministic AI services with fixed costs
Capitalized — transformed into economic deliverables (courses, strategies, products)
s1.pipeline_code:
Upload content → AI Extraction → Structured Neurons → AI Services → Monetizable Deliverables

Concrete example:
  1 podcast (60 min) → 1 episode → AI extraction → 5 neurons
  5 neurons → 10 AI services → 50+ deliverables
  50+ deliverables = articles, courses, strategies, frameworks, scripts
s1.philosophy: Philosophy: "Services First, Knowledge Always." The system doesn't generate random content — it executes deterministic services with defined input, fixed cost, and auditable output.
s1.positioning: The platform is "The Magic Marketing Button" — users upload content once, and the system automatically generates dozens of professional deliverables. The real margin comes from reuse: a framework extracted once can be reused 10,000 times.

s2.title: 2. Neuron Object — Data Model
s2.intro: The Neuron is the atomic unit of knowledge. Internally, it is a programmable document composed of typed blocks, with a three-level identity system:

s3.title: 3. Universal Block System
s3.intro: Blocks are the content primitives inside a Neuron. Each block has a type that determines rendering, execution behavior, and metadata schema. New types can be dynamically registered via the block_type_registry table.
types_rows: text, heading, subheading, markdown, todo, quote, list, idea, reference
s3.editor_desc: The Neuron editor is a hybrid block-based system — between Notion (structure), Google Docs (editing), VSCode (code), and Jupyter (execution). Supports insertion via slash command (/).

s4.title: 4. Content Classification
s4.intro: Each neuron is classified on two dimensions: content_category (what it contains) and lifecycle (maturity stage).
service_rows: Insight Extractor, Framework Detector, Quote Extractor
Course Generator, Strategy Builder, Prompt Generator
Market Research, Argument Mapper, Content Classifier

s5.title: 5. Relations & Knowledge Graph
s5.intro: Neurons form a directed graph through typed links. Each link has a semantic relation type that enables knowledge traversal, contradiction detection, and dependency tracking.
s5.nas_desc: NAS (Neuron Addressing System): Semantic coordinates that separate identity from location.

s6.title: 6. Git-like Versioning System
s6.intro: Each version stores a complete snapshot of the blocks with an optional diff from the parent. Supports branching, restoration, and tracking knowledge evolution.
ops_rows: Captures the current state of blocks as a new version
Reverts the neuron to any previous version
Compares two versions to see changes
Forks a version into a new chain for parallel editing

s7.title: 7. Execution Model & AI Services
s7.intro: Blocks can be executed depending on their execution mode. Services are deterministic AI pipelines with fixed costs and auditable output.
s7.name: Name
s7.realtime: Real-time monitoring via Supabase Realtime — the Jobs page updates automatically when any job status changes.

s8.title: 8. Credit Economy (NEURONS)
s8.callout: NOTA2 (on-chain, access & staking) + NEURONS (off-chain, compute credits for AI services)
s8.intro: The credit system implements an internal economy model with atomic reservation, auditing, and release on failure.
s8.flow_code:
1. Client → POST /run-service { job_id, service_key, neuron_id, inputs, user_id }
2. Server: Check balance ≥ service.credits_cost
3. Server: RESERVE — deduct credits atomically
4. Server: Log transaction (type: 'reserve')
5. Server: Execute AI via Lovable AI Gateway (SSE streaming)
6. Server: TEE stream → client (live) + audit (collect full result)
7. On SUCCESS:
   └─ Log transaction (type: 'spend')
   └─ Save result as neuron_block (type: 'markdown')
   └─ Update neuron lifecycle → 'structured'
   └─ Mark job 'completed'
   └─ Trigger notification → 'Job completed ✓'
8. On FAILURE:
   └─ RELEASE credits (restore balance)
   └─ Log transaction (type: 'release')
   └─ Mark job 'failed'
   └─ Trigger notification → 'Job failed ✗'

s9.title: 9. Ingestion & Extraction Pipeline
s9.intro: The complete pipeline from raw content to structured neurons:
s9.podcast_desc: Podcast Intelligence: The system extracts from podcasts: key segment identification, direct quote extraction, summary generation, and content classification. Episodes support types: text, audio, video, url.

s10.title: 10. Notification System
s10.callout: In-app notifications with Supabase Realtime + Desktop Notifications API + granular per-user preferences.
channels_rows: Supabase Realtime subscription on notifications table
Notification API — appears even if tab is not focused
s10.bell_desc: The NotificationBell component in the header displays a badge with unread notification count, a dropdown with preview, and a link to the /notifications page with type filtering and batch actions (mark all read, clear all).

s11.title: 11. Feedback & Testimonials System
s11.intro: Complete feedback collection system for testimonials, reviews, proposals, and complaints — with admin moderation workflow, Landing Page publication, and automatic contextual feedback.
collection_rows: Displays feedback marked as public by admin
s11.admin_code:
Feedback received → Automatic notification to all admins
                → Admin Dashboard tab "Feedback"
                → Stats: Total, Pending, Complaints, Average rating
                → Filter: type, status
                → Actions: change status, respond (→ user notification), 
                           publish/hide, export CSV
                → CSV Export: UTF-8 BOM, all columns, direct download

s12.title: 12. User Profiles & Public Pages
pages_rows: Edit display name, username, bio, avatar + notification preferences
Public page with user's public neurons
External resources directory with social proof
s12.settings_desc: The private profile includes notification settings sections: toggles per alert type, email digest frequency selection, quiet hours configuration.

s13.title: 13. Admin Dashboard
s13.callout: Route /admin protected by AdminRoute — checks 'admin' role via has_role() SECURITY DEFINER function. Roles are stored in a separate user_roles table (not on profile!).
tabs_rows: Global KPIs: total users, neurons, jobs, circulating credits, success rates
User list, role management (admin/moderator/user), credit adjustment
Execution monitoring, failure rates, average duration
Stats (total, pending, complaints, avg rating), filter, respond, publish, CSV export

s14.title: 14. Rules & Operating Principles
s14.callout: Services First · Job-centric Architecture · Invisible Library · Deterministic Execution · Real-time Feedback
principles_rows: Services First
Every AI interaction is a defined service with cost, input schema, and deliverables — not free chat
Job-centric Architecture
All work is tracked as auditable jobs: pending → running → completed/failed
Deterministic Execution
Same input + same service = predictable output structure. No randomness in pipeline
Credit Firewall
No execution without sufficient credits. Reservation before execution, release on failure
Invisible Library
The knowledge graph is implicit — neurons are connected but not exposed as a traditional library
Audit Trail
Every credit movement is logged: reserve, spend, release, denied. Complete transaction ledger
RLS Everywhere
Row-Level Security on all tables. Users only see their own data. Admins have separate policies
Lifecycle Progression
Neurons evolve: ingested → structured → active → capitalized → compounded
Block Composability
Any block type can be added to any neuron. New types registered dynamically
Version Immutability
Versions are append-only snapshots. History cannot be altered
Real-time Awareness
Instant notifications for jobs, credits, feedback — via Supabase Realtime + Desktop Notifications
Feedback Loop
Automatic contextual feedback after jobs, multiple collection points (FAB, inline, post-completion)
security_rows: Supabase Auth with email/password, email verification, session persistence
RLS policies per table; has_role() SECURITY DEFINER function
Separate user_roles table (admin, moderator, user) — NEVER on profile
Service role key for server-side operations; CORS headers
Server-side balance check and atomic deduction before AI execution
Users can read/modify/delete only their own notifications. INSERT only from server-side triggers
Users read their own feedback + public ones. Admins read all and can update

s15.title: 15. Cloning & Template System
s15.intro: Templates are pre-configured neuron structures. Cloning creates a deep copy with origin tracking.
ops_rows: Instantiates a new neuron with a pre-defined block structure
Deep copy of all blocks + metadata, with lineage tracking
Extracts the current neuron's structure as a reusable template

s16.title: 16. Application Pages & Flow
public_rows: Marketing, visual pipeline, public testimonials, CTA
External resources directory, social proof, live stats
Public page with user's public neurons
protected_rows: Main dashboard: stats, quick actions, recent neurons/jobs, pipeline hint
Edit existing neuron with preview, AI tools, versioning
Episode ingestion, transcript management, AI extraction
Execute service with inputs, SSE streaming, credit tracking
Execution history with status, duration, expandable results, realtime
Balance, transaction ledger, per-service consumption, top-up
Aggregate stats: neurons, episodes, categories, activity
Edit profile + notification preferences + quiet hours
Dedicated page: type filter, mark all read, clear all
Inline integrated form + own feedback history

s16.nav_desc: The user's cognitive pipeline reflected in navigation:

s18.title: 18. Edge Functions & API
s18.intro: Backend logic runs as Edge Functions on Lovable Cloud. All endpoints use CORS headers and service role authentication.
s18.neuron_api: CRUD for neurons, blocks, links, versions, search
s18.run_service: Job runner: credit reserve → AI execute → audit → save
s18.neuron_chat: Contextual AI chat in the neuron editor
